reforms.description: The Government proposes tax deductions for certain research and development costs in industrial firms in the period 1973-1980 to incentivize R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the tax burden on R&D expenditures, the reform encourages firms to invest more in innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposals for tax deductions were included in the government's budget statement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax deductions for R&D costs represents a significant policy shift aimed at stimulating private sector innovation, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Government proposes tax deductions for certain research and development costs in industrial firms in the period 1973-1980.
